quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure ('33.43% of revenue; that was 12.89% more than Widget's', 'North 53.83% higher', 'by 3.81 percentage points').

specificity.why: Every recommendation names specific products/regions with figures ('Prioritize Gadget: its 33.43% revenue share', 'Study North's sales mix', 'Address West and Sprocket together; their respective shares were lowest at 19.78% and 15.80%').

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple actionable observations beyond totals — Gadget's revenue concentration, the North–West gap, and pairing the two lowest-share segments (West and Sprocket) for joint attention.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure (e.g., "33.43% of revenue; that was 12.89% more than Widget's" and "North 53.83% higher").

specificity.why: Each recommendation names concrete products/regions with figures (e.g., "Prioritize Gadget: its 33.43% revenue share exceeded Widget's by 3.81 percentage points" and "Address West and Sprocket together").

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple actionable observations—revenue concentration, the North/West gap, and grouping the two lowest shares (West 19.78%, Sprocket 15.80%)—rather than just restating totals.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. Gadget was '12.89% more than Widget's $2,118.94' and North '53.83% higher' than West.

specificity.why: Every recommendation names concrete products/regions with figures, e.g. 'Prioritize Gadget: its 33.43% revenue share exceeded Widget's by 3.81 percentage points' and 'Address West and Sprocket together; their respective shares were lowest at 19.78% and 15.80%'.

insight_density.why: It surfaces concentration and gaps beyond raw totals—Gadget's 33.43% share, the 53.83% North-West gap, and grouping the two lowest performers (West and Sprocket).
